Meaning.En: having a small distance between two opposite surfaces or sides; not thick.
Meaning.Example: The paper is so thin that you can see through it.

Situation.Vi: Mô tả vật có độ dày nhỏ (giấy, vải, kính, bánh tráng).
Situation.En: Describing an object with minimal thickness (paper, fabric, glass, rice paper).

Meaning.En: (of a person) having little body fat or flesh; slim.
Meaning.Example: She’s very thin—she needs to eat more to gain some weight.

Situation.Vi: Mô tả người có thân hình gầy, thiếu mỡ.
Situation.En: Describing a person with little body fat or muscle mass.

Meaning.Vi: thưa thớt, ít (sự hiện diện, mật độ dân số, v.v.)
Meaning.En: (of people or things) sparse or scattered; not dense.
Meaning.Example: There was a thin crowd at the event.

Situation.Vi: Mô tả số lượng người hoặc vật ít ỏi, không đông.
Situation.En: Describing a small or sparse number of people or objects present.

Meaning.Vi: nhạt (nước soup, nước canh, nước sốt)
Meaning.En: (of a liquid, especially soup or sauce) lacking richness or strength of flavor; watery.
Meaning.Example: The soup was too thin—add more vegetables or a thickener.

Situation.Vi: Mô tả món ăn lỏng, thiếu độ sánh và hương vị đậm đà.
Situation.En: Describing a soup or sauce that lacks body and flavor intensity.
